nodes.content: This study investigates the very-high-cycle fatigue (VHCF) behavior at elevated temperature (650 °C) of the Inconel 718 alloy fabricated by selective laser melting (SLM).
nodes.context_or_evidence: The study focuses on the fatigue performance of Inconel 718 produced via SLM at 650°C, comparing the results with those of wrought alloy.

nodes.content: The machining process is characterized by high plastic deformation resistance, cutting load, and cutting temperature, therefore, machining of Inconel 718 via conventional methods is difficult.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Conventional machining of Inconel 718 is challenging due to its inherent properties that lead to high plastic deformation resistance, cutting load, and cutting temperature during machining.

nodes.content: This study presents a systematic investigation on the effect of volumetric energy density, and various LPBF processing parameters including laser power, layer thickness, and scanning speed on the as-printed specimens and the surface integrity aspects
nodes.context_or_evidence: The research examines how volumetric energy density, laser power, layer thickness, and scanning speed influence the surface integrity of Inconel 718 specimens produced by LPBF.

nodes.content: After evaluating, predicting and comparing LCF life, the hysteretic energy model related to temperature is proven to an effective approach.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The hysteretic energy model, when temperature-dependent, is an effective method for evaluating, predicting, and comparing low cycle fatigue (LCF) life.

nodes.content: The damage mechanisms at 25, 450 and 650 °C can be explained by combining the c
nodes.context_or_evidence: Damage mechanisms in Selective Laser Melted Inconel 718 at 25°C, 450°C, and 650°C can be understood by combining the...

nodes.content: long life fatigue ... and solution-aged Inconel 718 superalloy fabricated byselective laser melting (SLM) at service temperature of 650 °C.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The research addresses long life fatigue in solution-aged Inconel 718 superalloy produced via selective laser melting (SLM) at a service temperature of 650°C.

nodes.content: Pores in Selective Laser Melting (SLM) Inconel 718 specimens are attributed to entrapped gas during the SLM process.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The entrapped gas originates either from the gas present in the initial powders or from the inert atmosphere (e.g., argon) used during the SLM process.

nodes.title: FATIGUE CRACK INITIATION IN ALLOY 718 AT 650°C

nodes.content: Fatigue crack initiation in Inconel 718 is influenced by notch surface condition at 650°C.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The notch surface condition can dominate the crack initiation life in Alloy 718 at 650°C. Modified heat treatment can create a notch-sensitive microstructure, leading to a two order of magnitude loss in high cycle fatigue life.

nodes.content: Creep cracking tests were performed on Inconel 718 at 650°C in air using CT type specimens.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Time to initiate creep crack propagation (T1) and creep crack growth rate (ȧ) were measured as a function of stress.

nodes.content: In-situ re-scanning during Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) deepens melt pools and forms a stalactite-like morphology in Inconel 718.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This morphological change is a direct result of the in-situ re-scanning process during LPBF.

nodes.content: Melt pool boundaries (MPB) guide fatigue crack propagation in Inconel 718 fabricated by LPBF.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The melt pool boundary (MPB) acted as a guide to the propagation of fatigue cracks in vertically formed specimens.

nodes.content: In-situ re-scanning improves fatigue crack growth (FCG) resistance of vertically formed Inconel 718 specimens.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The in-situ re-scanning can improve the FCG resistance of the specimens with vertical forming.

nodes.content: In-situ re-scanning leads to dynamic recrystallization, smaller Laves precipitates, and refined grains in LPBF-fabricated Inconel 718.
nodes.context_or_evidence: After in-situ re-scanning, dynamic recrystallization occurred, Laves precipitates displayed low sizes, and the grains were effectively refined.

nodes.content: Fatigue strength of selective laser melted Inconel 718 at 650°C was enhanced by ~50% after surface machining and polishing.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The effects of surface roughness and build thickness on fatigue properties of selective laser melted Inconel 718 were investigated at 650°C.

nodes.content: Surfaces of additively manufactured Inconel 718 display high roughness and defects that act as fatigue initiation sites.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The fatigue behavior of notched and unnotched specimens produced by additively manufactured Inconel 718 were analyzed in the as-built and heat-treated conditions.

nodes.content: Solution treated and aged, polished Inconel 718 reaches a high cycle fatigue regime within the stress amplitude range of 500 to 600 MPa.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Available literature shows that solution treated and aged, polished Inconel 718 reaches a high cycle fatigue regime within the stress amplitude range of 500 to 600 MPa.

nodes.content: Metal parts obtained by layer-wise selective melting of a powder bed are characterized by surfaces that are considerably rougher than conventionally machined parts.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Original fatigue data of L-PBF Inconel 718 obtained with specimens after individual surface treatments.

nodes.content: Fatigue crack initiation in Alloy 718 was studied at 650°C.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Fatigue crack initiation in Alloy 718 at 650°C was investigated by R. G. Andrews, A. K. Koul and P. Au.

nodes.content: Fatigue strength of selective laser melted Inconel 718 at 650°C was enhanced by approximately 50% after surface machining and polishing.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The study investigated the effects of surface roughness and build thickness on fatigue properties of selective laser melted Inconel 718 at 650°C.

nodes.content: Strain-controlled thermomechanical fatigue tests were performed on Inconel 718 in conventional and electron-beam-melted (EBM) form to evaluate the potential of additively manufactured superalloys for high-temperature components.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The study compares the thermomechanical fatigue performance of Inconel 718 produced by conventional methods and electron beam melting.

nodes.content: Additive manufacturing of nickel-based superalloys like Inconel 718 via electron beam melting (EBM) is gaining attention for aerospace applications.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The study focuses on challenges related to surface hardness and high-temperature wear resistance in EBM-produced Inconel 718.

nodes.content: Post-processing EBM Inconel 718 enhances mechanical properties in both directions and at room temperature and 650°C.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The study investigates the mechanical behavior of post-processed EBM Inconel 718.

nodes.content: Post-processing decreases the spatial variance of the ultimate yield strength and elongation at failure of EBM Inconel 718.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The study examines the impact of post-processing on the mechanical properties of EBM Inconel 718.

nodes.content: Inconel 718 is widely used in gas turbine blades, aero engines, and other high temperature (HT) structural parts due to its weldability, manufacturability, and mechanical properties.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The study focuses on the room and high temperature high-cycle fatigue properties of Inconel 718.

nodes.content: Metal parts obtained by layer-wise selective melting of a powder bed have surfaces that are considerably rougher than conventionally machined parts.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The study investigates the fatigue behavior of as-built L-PBF Inconel 718 and the impact of surface roughness.

nodes.content: Shot peening can be implemented on various materials to introduce compressive residual stress, which can improve fatigue life.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Surface treatments such as shot peening can be implemented on various materials, which, by introducing compressive residual stress, can improve fatigue life, especially in components subjected to time-varying stresses in energy systems.

nodes.content: Surface compressive residual stress enhances the resistance of IN718 alloy to plastic deformation and closes short cracks.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Surface compressive residual stress tended to close short cracks and enhance the resistance of IN718 alloy to plastic deformation.

nodes.content: Shot peening treatment significantly improves fatigue life of IN718 alloy.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Klotz et al. and Wu et al. both found that shot peening treatment significantly improves fatigue life.

nodes.content: A study investigates the very-high-cycle fatigue (VHCF) behavior at elevated temperature (650 °C) of the Inconel 718 alloy fabricated by selective laser melting (SLM).
nodes.context_or_evidence: This study investigates the very-high-cycle fatigue (VHCF) behavior at elevated temperature (650 °C) of the Inconel 718 alloy fabricated by selective laser melting (SLM).

nodes.content: Fatigue tests on INCONEL alloy 718 were conducted at 20kHz frequency, room temperature, and stress ratio R = -1.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Fatigue test S-N curve of INCONEL alloy 718 at f 20kHz, room temperature, and stress ratio R = -1

nodes.content: AM Inconel 718 mechanical properties were evaluated in two build orientations using an EOS M290 printer.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This study evaluates the mechanical properties of AM Inconel 718 in two build orientations produced using an EOS M290 printer.

nodes.content: Static tests and hardness measurements were conducted to establish baseline properties of AM Inconel 718.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Static tests and hardness measurements were conducted to establish baseline properties.

nodes.title: Analytical fatigue life prediction of shot peened Inconel 718

nodes.content: Shot peening of Inconel 718 affects crack initiation and propagation.
nodes.context_or_evidence: crack initiation and propagation in shot peened Inconel 718 for different shot peening conditions

nodes.content: NCBI is modernizing its website to improve user experience in finding and accessing resources.
nodes.context_or_evidence: NCBI, part of the National Library of Medicine (NLM), is excited to announce our new Beta homepage! It represents the first phase of a multi-year effort to modernize many pages on our website. We are working to improve your experience so you can find and access NCBI resources quickly and easily.

nodes.content: Shot peening introduces compressive residual stresses and work hardening at the surface of mechanical parts.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Shot peening is commonly used on mechanical parts submitted to cyclic loads. During the process, compressive residual stresses and work hardening are introduced at the shot peened surface [1].

nodes.content: Compressive residual stresses and work hardening induced by shot peening delay crack initiation and propagation, increasing fatigue life.
nodes.context_or_evidence: These two factors delay crack initiation and propagation and thus increase fatigue life.

nodes.content: Shot peening can modify crack initiation mechanisms and relocate crack initiation sites from the surface to the subsurface.
nodes.context_or_evidence: It is also reported that shot peening can modify the crack initiation mechanisms and relocate the crack initiation sites from the surface to the subsurface [2,3].

nodes.content: Shot peening increases surface roughness, which can result in stress concentrations that alter fatigue life if parameters are not carefully chosen.
nodes.context_or_evidence: However, shot peening increases the surface roughness [4] which results in stress concentrations that can alter the fatigue life if the shot peening parameters are not carefully chosen [5,6].

nodes.content: In the aerospace industry, shot peening is often considered a safety margin rather than accounted for in the design stage.
nodes.context_or_evidence: In the aerospace industry, shot peening is usually not accounted for in the design stage but is rather considered as a safety margin.

nodes.content: Modeling shot peening effects on crack initiation and propagation could lead to lighter parts and reduce trials and errors in defining optimal peening parameters.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Understanding and modeling shot peening effects on crack initiation and propagation mechanisms would provide the essential grounds to account for the process beneficial effects at the design stage. This would lead to considerably lighter parts and would reduce the trials and errors required to define optimal peening parameters.

nodes.content: Ultrasonic Nanocrystal Surface Modification (UNSM) is a mechanical surface treatment that induces large compressive residual stresses and near-surface microstructural changes.
nodes.context_or_evidence: UltrasonicNanocrystalSurfaceModification(UNSM) is a mechanicalsurfacetreatment that induces large compressive residual stresses and near-surfacemicrostructural changes in the material using repetitive impacts atultrasonicfrequencies with a WC tip.

nodes.content: UNSM treatment at room and high temperatures affects the high-frequency fatigue behavior of Inconel 718 alloy fabricated by laser metal deposition (LMD).
nodes.context_or_evidence: In this work, the effect ofultrasonicnanocrystalsurfacemodification(UNSM) treatment at room and high temperatures (RT and HT) on the high-frequencyfatiguebehavior ofInconel718alloy fabricated by laser metal deposition (LMD) process was experimentally

nodes.title: PDFRotary Bending Fatigue and Seizure Characteristics of Inconel 718 alloy ...

nodes.content: UNSM improves mechanical fatigue and tribological properties of Inconel 718 alloy.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This study reports theimprovementsin mechanicalfatigueand tribological properties of commercially availableInconel718alloy subjected to anultrasonicnanocrystallinesurfacemodification(UNSM) technique.

nodes.content: UNSM induces similar changes in the surface and subsurface of annealed Inconel 718 alloy to improve fatigue performance.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This study investigates the ability ofultrasonicnanocrystalsurfacemodification(UNSM), a novelsurfacetreatment technology, to induce similar changes in thesurfaceand subsurface of the annealedInconel718alloy to improvefatigueperformance.

nodes.content: Shot peening introduces compressive residual stresses and work hardening at the surface of mechanical parts.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a common effect of shot peening on mechanical parts subjected to cyclic loads, delaying crack initiation and propagation, thus increasing fatigue life.

nodes.content: Shot peening can modify crack initiation mechanisms and relocate crack initiation sites from the surface to the subsurface.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Reported effect of shot peening on crack initiation, potentially altering the location where cracks begin.

nodes.content: Shot peening increases surface roughness, which can lead to stress concentrations that alter fatigue life if parameters are not carefully chosen.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Increased surface roughness due to shot peening can negatively impact fatigue life if shot peening parameters are not optimized to avoid stress concentrations.

nodes.content: In the aerospace industry, shot peening is often considered a safety margin rather than accounted for in the design stage.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Current practice in the aerospace industry where shot peening benefits are not explicitly included in the initial design calculations.

nodes.content: Modeling shot peening effects on crack initiation and propagation could lead to lighter parts and reduce trials and errors in defining optimal peening parameters.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Potential benefits of accurately modeling shot peening's impact on crack behavior, enabling more efficient design processes.

nodes.content: The study aims at modeling crack initiation and propagation in shot peened Inconel 718 for different shot peening conditions and applied stress levels.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The objective of the research is to create a model for predicting crack behavior in Inconel 718 under varying shot peening treatments and stress conditions.

nodes.content: Thierry Klotz, Dorian Delbergue, Hong Yan Miao, Philippe Bocher, Martin Lévesque, and Myriam Brochu are authors involved in the research on analytical fatigue life prediction of shot peened Inconel 718.
nodes.context_or_evidence: These individuals are credited as authors of the study, indicating their involvement in the research and analysis.

nodes.content: École Polytechnique de Montréal and École de Technologie Supérieure in Canada are institutions involved in the research.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The affiliations of the authors indicate that these institutions are contributing to the research on shot peening and fatigue life prediction.

nodes.content: Ultrasonic Nanocrystal Surface Modification (UNSM) technique improves mechanical fatigue and tribological properties of Inconel 718 alloy.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This study reports the improvements in mechanical fatigue and tribological properties of commercially available Inconel 718 alloy subjected to an ultrasonic nanocrystalline surface modification (UNSM) technique.

nodes.content: Fatigue life of additively manufactured Inconel 718 can be improved up to 75-fold compared to the as-built state after applying LSP + UNSM treatments.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The fatigue life was improved up to 75-fold compared to the as-built state after applying LSP + UNSM treatments.

nodes.content: Fatigue life of additively manufactured Inconel 718 can be improved up to 56-fold compared to the as-built state after applying UNSM treatment.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The fatigue life was improved up to 56-fold compared to the as-built state after applying UNSM treatments.

nodes.content: Fatigue life of additively manufactured Inconel 718 can be improved up to 35-fold compared to the as-built state after applying LSP treatment.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The fatigue life was improved up to 35-fold compared to the as-built state after applying LSP treatments.

nodes.content: Inconel 718 exhibits excellent creep-rupture properties up to around 650°C.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Inconel 718: excellent creep-rupture properties up to around 650°C (≈1300°F)

nodes.content: Inconel 718 is widely used up to approximately 700°C for some formats.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Inconel 718: widely used up to ~700°C for some formats

nodes.content: Shot peening is widely used in the aerospace industry to improve the fatigue life of critical components made of Inconel 718.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Surface enhancement methods, such as shot peening, are widely used in the aerospace industry to improve the fatigue life of critical components.

nodes.content: Inconel 718 is manufactured according to the NACE MR0175 standard and is classified as type 4d, indicating its resistance to various combinations and concentrations of chlorides and hydrogen sulfide.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Сплав изготавливается по стандарту NACE MR0175 и регламентируется как тип 4d, что говорит о его устойчивости к различным комбинациям и концентрациям хлоридов и сероводорода.

nodes.content: Ultrasonic Surface Nano-crystallization Modification (UNSM) treatment is applied to Inconel 718 alloys to improve their high-temperature ultrasonic fatigue performance.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The study investigates the ultrasonic fatigue performance of high-temperature structural material Inconel 718 alloys at high temperature after UNSM treatment.

nodes.content: Shot peening is a surface enhancement method widely used in the aerospace industry to improve the fatigue life of critical components.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Shot peening generates residual compressive stresses which tend to delay fatigue crack initiation and prevent small crack propagation, making it a valuable technique in aerospace.

nodes.content: Shot peening induces residual compressive stresses that delay fatigue crack initiation and prevent small crack propagation.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The process generates residual compressive stresses which tend to delay fatigue crack initiation and prevent small crack propagation.

nodes.content: The effectiveness of shot peening depends on the thermo-mechanical relaxation of residual stresses at operating temperatures.
nodes.context_or_evidence: These methods are efficient only if the thermo-mechanical relaxation of residual stresses at operating temperatures is moderate.

nodes.content: Work hardening induced by shot peening is strongly related to the microstructure of the material.
nodes.context_or_evidence: There is a strong dependence of the degree of work hardening induced by creation of the compressive layer on the amount of relaxation during thermal loadings. Furthermore, work hardening is strongly related to the microstructure.

nodes.content: The microstructural state of the component part before shot peening can significantly modify the efficiency of the surface enhancement method and the fatigue life of the component.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The microstructural state of the component part before shot peening can then significantly modify the efficiency of this surface enhancement method and the fatigue life of the component.

nodes.content: The study aims to assess the impact of shot-peening on the fatigue life of IN718 turbine disks.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The present study aims at assessing the impact of shot-peening on fatigue life of IN718 turbine disk.

nodes.content: Residual stresses induced by shot peening in IN718 can be thermally relaxed by aging in a temperature range up to 750°C for aging times between 0.1 and 100 hours.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Shotpeened specimens were aged in a temperature range up to 750°C for aging times between 0.1 and 100 hours. The time dependent changes in the residual stress distribution were determined experimentally using X-ray diffraction.

nodes.content: Thermal annealing affects machining-induced residual stresses in Inconel 718.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This paper studies the effect of thermal loads on machining-induced residual stresses in the alloy Inconel 718. A ring-shaped Inconel 718 part was fac

nodes.content: Residual stresses and work hardening induced by shot peening are only partially relaxed during thermal loading, even for the longest holding times.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The residual stresses and work hardening induced by shot peening are only partially relaxed during thermal loading, even for the longest holding times.
